perturbation.original_text: "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person, any other person directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with such person and, in the case of a trust, any trustee or beneficiary (actual or potential) of that trust and, in the case of an individual, any person connected with him; provided that, for the purposes of this Agreement, neither QIWI nor any of its subsidiaries is to be regarded as an Affiliate of Otkritie;
perturbation.changed_text: "Affiliate" means, with respect to any person, any other person directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with such person and, in the case of a trust, any trustee or beneficiary (actual or potential) of that trust and, in the case of an individual, any person connected with him;
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'provided that, for the purposes of this Agreement, neither QIWI nor any of its subsidiaries is to be regarded as an Affiliate of Otkritie;' creates an in-text contradiction, because further down the document, in the 5.2 (b) section, it is stated 'to its Affiliates and the directors, officers, employees, agents, and professional advisers or its and/or their Affiliates (the 'Representatives') on a need to know basis to the extent reasonably required for purposes connected with this Agreement;' QIWI may disclose confidential information if deemed affiliate of Otkritie. This change makes it ambiguous whether QIWI is considered an affiliate for the purposes of confidential information sharing under section 5.2(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality in clause 5.1 do not apply in respect of the disclosing of Confidential Information in the following circumstances: (a) in connection with the performance of each party's obligations hereunder or the enforcement of its rights hereunder; (b) to its Affiliates and the directors, officers, employees, agents, and professional advisers or its and/or their Affiliates (the"Representatives") on a need to know basis to the extent reasonably required for purposes connected with this Agreement; (c) pursuant to any listing agreement with or the rules and regulations of any recognised security exchange on which securities of suchParty or any of its Affiliates are listed and/or traded; (d) as required by Applicable Law, provided in each case set out in Clauses 5.2(a) and 5.2(b) above, the Party disclosing the same shall take all reasonable steps to preserve the confidentiality thereof and to ensure that such information shall be used only for the purposes for which it has been disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the clause excluding QIWI and its subsidiaries from being considered affiliates of Otkritie creates a direct contradiction with section 5.2(b). Without the exclusion, QIWI could be considered an affiliate, allowing it to disclose confidential information under 5.2(b). This directly contradicts the implied intention of the original agreement to restrict QIWI's access to confidential information related to Otkritie by explicitly not regarding them as affiliates.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 The Steering Committee shall meet at least once in each month and as otherwise agreed between Committee Member. Such meetings shall take place in Moscow, in a location agreed by Otkritie and QIWI Group or by telephone.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 The Steering Committee shall meet at least once in each month and as otherwise agreed between Committee Member.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'Such meetings shall take place in Moscow, in a location agreed by Otkritie and QIWI Group or by telephone.' creates an in-text contradiction with 3.5 'The Steering Committee shall validly act only at a duly convened meeting with the approval of at least four (4) of the Committee Members present at such meeting.' since it doesn't give the means on how it can be applied if they don't meet or do so by telephone.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Steering Committee shall validly act only at a duly convened meeting with the approval of at least four (4) of the Committee Members present at such meeting. Upon the approval of a proposal by the Steering Committee, the Steering Committee shall present such proposal to the respective governing body of each Otkritie and of QIWI for approval. For the avoidance of doubt, no decision of the Steering Committee shall be binding on the Parties unless, and the Parties are only required to act upon any decision of the Steering Committee if and to the extent that, such decision is properly approved/ratified by the relevant governing bodies of both Otkritie and QIWI.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'Such meetings shall take place in Moscow, in a location agreed by Otkritie and QIWI Group or by telephone.' from section 3.3 directly contradicts section 3.5, which states, 'The Steering Committee shall validly act only at a duly convened meeting with the approval of at least four (4) of the Committee Members present at such meeting.' The original text provided a means for the meeting to occur (in person or by telephone), allowing section 3.5 to be fulfilled. By removing this, the contract provides no guidance on how a 'duly convened meeting' can take place, thus making the approval process described in 3.5 unenforceable as it is unclear how the meeting can occur which creates a strong inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 A notice (including any approval, consent or other communication) in connection with this Agreement and the documents referred to in it or with any arbitration under this Agreement: (a) must be in writing in English; (b) for the avoidance of doubt, must not be sent by electronic mail; (c) must be delivered by hand, fax or courier using an internationally recognised courier company to the address specified below in relation to the Party to whom the notice is addressed, and marked for the attention of the person so specified, or to such other address or fax number or marked for the attention of such other person, as the relevant party may from time to time specify by notice given in accordance with this clause.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 A notice (including any approval, consent or other communication) in connection with this Agreement and the documents referred to in it or with any arbitration under this Agreement: (a) must be in writing in English; (c) must be delivered by hand, fax or courier using an internationally recognised courier company to the address specified below in relation to the Party to whom the notice is addressed, and marked for the attention of the person so specified, or to such other address or fax number or marked for the attention of such other person, as the relevant party may from time to time specify by notice given in accordance with this clause.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'for the avoidance of doubt, must not be sent by electronic mail;' this creates an in-text contradiction with 8.3, because the original text doesn't specify how to handle an email, since it excludes the option. This leaves the reader not knowing what happens if they send an email.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A notice is deemed to be received: (a) in the case of a notice delivered by hand or courier at the address of the addressee given above, upon delivery at that address; and (b) in the case of a notice delivered by fax, at the time of transmission (provided that a transmission report from the machine from which thefax was sent is received by the sender which indicates that the fax was sent in its entirety to the fax number of the recipient).
perturbation.justification: The omission of the phrase 'for the avoidance of doubt, must not be sent by electronic mail' from section 8.1 creates a direct contradiction with the stipulated methods of notice delivery outlined in the contradicted text. The contradicted text specifies the conditions under which a notice is deemed to be received for hand delivery, courier, and fax, but now fails to specify how email is received. As 8.1, as changed, now allows email, there is no means in the contradicted section for deeming when the email was received. This creates a strong contradiction, making it uncertain how the parties should account for email.
